title: Overcoming Fears, Fixing Problems, and Travel Challenges

welcomeMessage.en: Today, we are talking about facing our fears and dealing with difficult situations.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天我们将谈论面对恐惧和处理困境。

welcomeMessage.en: We'll discuss the emotions of learning a language, being afraid of making mistakes, and what to do during a travel emergency.
welcomeMessage.zh: 我们将讨论学习语言的情绪，害怕犯错误，以及在旅行紧急情况下该怎么做。

welcomeMessage.en: Get ready to learn how to confidently fix problems in English!
welcomeMessage.zh: 准备好学习如何自信地用英语解决问题！

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is something that you used to be afraid of, but are not afraid of anymore?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 有什么事情是你曾经害怕，但现在不再害怕的？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Do you often feel worried or want to cry when English feels too difficult? How do you overcome it?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 当英语感觉太难时，你是否经常感到担心或想哭？你是如何克服它的？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: If you are traveling alone and someone is annoying you on a flight, what would you do?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 如果你独自旅行，航班上有人打扰你，你会怎么做？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: When a TV or machine is broken, do you usually try to fix it yourself or ask for help?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 当电视或机器坏了时，你通常会尝试自己修理还是寻求帮助？

vocabulary.definition: Mend or repair something that is broken.
vocabulary.example: If the TV on the airplane is broken, you can ask the flight attendant to fix it.

vocabulary.definition: Say something in order to obtain an answer or some information.
vocabulary.example: If you are lost and traveling alone, you should ask a police officer for help.

vocabulary.definition: A matter or situation regarded as unwelcome or harmful.
vocabulary.example: We encountered a huge problem when our flight was suddenly canceled.
Traveling alone can be scary, but it also makes you very independent.

vocabulary.definition: Anxious or troubled about actual or potential problems.
vocabulary.example: I was very worried that my English was not good enough for the trip.

vocabulary.definition: Make someone a little angry; irritate.
vocabulary.example: It really annoys me when people talk loudly on their phones during a flight.
You will never improve if you are afraid of making mistakes when speaking.

vocabulary.definition: An action or judgment that is misguided or wrong.
vocabulary.example: Making a grammar mistake is completely normal; don't let it upset you!

vocabulary.definition: The action or process of flying through the air.
vocabulary.example: My flight from Beijing to New York takes over 14 hours.

vocabulary.definition: Needing much effort or skill to accomplish.
vocabulary.example: Memorizing all of this new English vocabulary is very hard, but you can do it!

vocabulary.definition: The body of words used in a particular language.
vocabulary.example: Reading English books is the best way to expand your vocabulary.

vocabulary.definition: Shed tears, typically as an expression of distress.
vocabulary.example: The movie's ending was so sad that it made everyone in the theater cry.

sessionSummary: Today's class was all about facing our fears and dealing with difficult situations! We had a very honest discussion about the emotions of learning a language and traveling.

We talked about how it is completely normal to feel worried or to even want to cry when English feels too hard or difficult. A big topic today was being 'afraid of' making mistakes. Everyone agreed that we need to overcome this fear because making mistakes is how we grow our vocabulary!

We also discussed travel scenarios, like what to do if you are traveling alone on a flight and encounter a problem. We practiced how to confidently ask someone to fix the issue, even if you are worried about annoying them.

Finally, instead of doing our roleplay in class today, we have assigned a special roleplay scenario as homework! Make sure to review the instructions at the bottom of these notes before our next session.

keyVocabulary.example: "If the TV on the airplane is broken, you can ask the flight attendant to fix it."
"If you are lost and traveling alone, you should ask a police officer for help."
"We encountered a huge problem when our flight was suddenly canceled."
"Traveling alone can be scary, but it also makes you very independent."
"I was very worried that my English was not good enough for the trip."
"It really annoys me when people talk loudly on their phones during a flight."
"You will never improve if you are afraid of making mistakes when speaking."
"Making a grammar mistake is completely normal; don't let it upset you!"
"My flight from Beijing to New York takes over 14 hours."
"Memorizing all of this new English vocabulary is very hard, but you can do it!"
"Reading English books is the best way to expand your vocabulary."
"The movie's ending was so sad that it made everyone in the theater cry."

grammarTips.title: Learn vs. Study: What's the difference?
grammarTips.description: These two words are often confused! 'Study' is the PROCESS of trying to understand something (like reading a book or reviewing notes). 'Learn' is the RESULT of studying (actually gaining the knowledge or skill).
grammarTips.incorrect: "I learned for my exam for three hours last night."
grammarTips.correct: "I STUDIED for my exam for three hours, and I LEARNED a lot of new vocabulary."

grammarTips.title: Hard vs. Difficult
grammarTips.description: Both words mean 'not easy' and can usually be used interchangeably. However, 'hard' can also describe a physical object (like a hard rock), while 'difficult' is only used for situations, tasks, or concepts.
grammarTips.correct: "This chair is very HARD. / This math problem is very DIFFICULT (or HARD)."

grammarTips.title: Review: Afraid of + Noun / Verb-ing
grammarTips.description: Remember our rule from a few days ago! When using 'afraid of', you must follow it with a noun or an '-ing' verb.

homeworkRoleplay.title: Day 11 Homework: Flight Problem Roleplay
homeworkRoleplay.description: Please prepare for the following roleplay scenario. We will perform this at the beginning of Day 11's class!
homeworkRoleplay.scenario: You are traveling alone on a 12-hour international flight. The passenger sitting next to you is annoying you (playing loud music), and the TV screen on your seat is broken. You feel a bit worried and want to cry, but you must overcome your fear of making mistakes in English. You press the call button to ask the flight attendant for help.

roles.Student A (Passenger): Politely ask the flight attendant to fix the TV problem and handle the annoying passenger.
roles.Student B (Flight Attendant): Listen to the problem, apologize, and try to fix the situation for the passenger.

homeworkRoleplay.preparationTip: Think about how to use words like 'problem', 'fix', 'annoy', and 'worried' in your dialogue!

goals: Ensure students stop using 'learn' incorrectly and switch to 'study'.
Help students overcome their fear of making mistakes.

phases.title: Phase 1: Emotions of Language Learning (15-20 Minutes)
phases.description: Discuss the difficulties of learning English and feeling worried.
questions: Do you often feel worried or want to cry when English feels too difficult? How do you overcome it?

phases.title: Phase 2: Fixing Problems (20-30 Minutes)
phases.description: Discuss dealing with annoyances while traveling alone.
questions: If you are traveling alone and someone is annoying you on a flight, what would you do?

phases.title: Phase 3: Homework Assignment (10 Minutes)
phases.description: Explain the roleplay scenario and ensure everyone understands their roles.
